document_text:
PATIENT CONFIDENTIALITY FORM — MERIDIAN MEDICAL CENTER

Patient Jonathan M. Richardson, DOB March 14, 1978, was admitted on April 22, 2026 to Meridian Medical Center, San Francisco. Coverage is confirmed under policy BC-2847391-MH with SSN 287-41-9630 on file. The patient is married and authorized release of PHI per HIPAA §164.506. Designated emergency contact is reachable at (415) 555-0142; primary email on file is j.richardson@meridian-health.org. Mailing address: 1847 Divisadero St, San Francisco, CA 94115. Attending physician is Dr. Elena Vasquez (NPI 1093847562). Record ID MRN-008473. Patient self-identifies as Hispanic.

spans.reason: High-confidence personal name match; replaced with [NAME_1] token, recoverable via the local mapping.
Matched a date pattern adjacent to a date-of-birth label; date-of-birth is a quasi-identifier.
City-level location flagged as low-risk; common enough that re-identification risk was assessed as low. Judgment call, not certainty.
Matched strict alphanumeric policy-number pattern; permanently removed, not recoverable.
Matched the standard XXX-XX-XXXX Social Security Number pattern; deterministic, permanently removed.
Flagged as potentially sensitive demographic info, but confidence was below threshold. Borderline — worth a manual look.
Matched as a legal/regulatory citation, not personal data; confidently cleared rather than just unflagged.
Matched standard US phone format; high deterministic confidence. Anonymized with a recoverable token.
Matched standard email pattern; anonymized so downstream processing can still reference the same person.
Full mailing address combines a street, city and ZIP — a direct identifier; permanently removed.
Matched as a person's name, but appears to be a treating clinician rather than the patient. Kept visible — borderline, review if sharing externally.
Matched 10-digit National Provider Identifier pattern; anonymized to preserve referential integrity.
Matched the facility's MRN prefix and digit-length pattern; permanently removed.
Flagged as a protected demographic attribute; left visible because the surrounding text is a self-identification statement. Borderline — review.
